input.description:
Use this tool when:
The user asks to start, create, or save a draft email (new email or reply).
The user asks to write an email that may be sent now or later.
The user asks to send an email and no draft has been created or explicitly referenced earlier in the current conversational context (i.e., a draft that has been created, retrieved, or explicitly mentioned in the conversation).

Reply-to-Thread Rules
For reply and reply-all, infer recipients strictly from the original message or thread context.
When replying to a thread, omit the To field and rely on thread metadata for recipient resolution.
For reply-all, preserve the original Cc and Bcc recipients as present in the source message.
For replies, retrieve all required fields using the get_message tool.
Subject, Cc, and Bcc must exactly match the original email.
For reply drafts, threadId, inReplyTo, and references are mandatory.
The subject must remain unchanged from the original email.
The reply must maintain proper context from the original message.

Do not use this tool to add attachments (use add_attachments after creating the draft). If the user wants attachments, create the draft first, then call add_attachments.

properties.label: To
properties.name: to
properties.hint: Use for new drafts/reply-to or when the user explicitly specifies recipients; omit for thread replies unless explicitly overridden.

properties.label: Cc
properties.name: cc
properties.hint: CC recipients must be included only when explicitly specified by the user; CCs should never be inferred or auto-expanded. For reply and reply-all actions, recipients must be derived strictly from the original message or thread context.

properties.label: Bcc
properties.name: bcc
properties.hint: BCC recipients must be explicitly provided. For reply-all actions, BCC recipients are taken from the original message or thread context and combined with any BCC addresses the user adds, without duplication. For all other cases, only the BCC recipients explicitly provided by the user are used—no inference or auto-addition applies.

properties.label: Subject
properties.name: subject
properties.hint: Infer for new drafts if missing; for replies, it is mandatory to set the same subject as the original email.

properties.label: Body
properties.name: body
properties.hint: Required: write the full email content exactly as intended; do not add signatures, disclaimers, or quoted history unless explicitly requested.

properties.label: Body format
properties.name: bodyFormat
properties.hint: Default to plain_text; use html only when the user provides HTML or explicitly wants rich formatting.

properties.label: Thread ID
properties.name: threadId
properties.hint: Set only when creating a reply/reply-all draft so the draft is associated with the correct Gmail thread; omit for new standalone drafts.

properties.label: In reply to
properties.name: inReplyTo
properties.hint: Mandatory for replies, set to the original Message-ID header value (e.g., <abc@domain>) to preserve threading; omit for new drafts.

properties.label: References
properties.name: references
properties.hint: Mandatory for replies, set to the original References header (plus the original Message-ID if needed) to preserve threading; omit for new drafts.

input.code:
require "base64"
require "securerandom"
require "time"

normalize_list = lambda do |v|
  return [] if v.nil?
  arr = v.is_a?(Array) ? v : [v]
  arr.map(&:to_s).map(&:strip).reject(&:empty?)
end

crlf = lambda do |s|
  s.to_s.gsub("\r\n", "\n").gsub("\r", "\n").gsub("\n", "\r\n")
end

base64url_encode = lambda do |str|
  Base64.urlsafe_encode64(str, padding: false)
end

safe_param = lambda do |v|
  v.to_s.gsub(/[\r\n]/, "").gsub('"', '\"').strip
end

chunk76 = lambda do |b64|
  b64.to_s.scan(/.{1,76}/).join("\r\n")
end

html_to_text_fallback = lambda do |html|
  html.to_s
      .gsub(/<br\s*\/?>/i, "\n")
      .gsub(/<\/p>/i, "\n\n")
      .gsub(/<[^>]+>/, "")
      .strip
end

build_body_part = lambda do |body, body_format|
  fmt = (body_format || "plain_text").to_s
  case fmt
  when "plain_text"
    [
      "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"UTF-8\"",
      "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit",
      "",
      crlf.call(body),
      ""
    ].join("\r\n")
  when "html"
    boundary = "alt-#{SecureRandom.hex(8)}"
    plain = html_to_text_fallback.call(body)
    [
      "Content-Type: multipart/alternative; boundary=\"#{boundary}\"",
      "",
      "--#{boundary}",
      "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"UTF-8\"",
      "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit",
      "",
      crlf.call(plain),
      "",
      "--#{boundary}",
      "Content-Type: text/html; charset=\"UTF-8\"",
      "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit",
      "",
      crlf.call(body),
      "",
      "--#{boundary}--",
      ""
    ].join("\r\n")
  else
    raise "Invalid body_format: #{fmt}. Use plain_text or html."
  end
end

to  = normalize_list.call(input["to"])
cc  = normalize_list.call(input["cc"])
bcc = normalize_list.call(input["bcc"])

subject = input["subject"].to_s.strip
subject = "(no subject)" if subject.empty?

body = input["body"].to_s
body_format = input["body_format"].to_s.strip
body_format = "plain_text" if body_format.empty?

thread_id   = input["thread_id"].to_s.strip
in_reply_to = input["in_reply_to"].to_s.strip
references  = input["references"].to_s.strip

body_part = build_body_part.call(body, body_format)

headers = []
headers << "To: #{to.join(', ')}" if to.any?
headers << "Cc: #{cc.join(', ')}" if cc.any?
headers << "Bcc: #{bcc.join(', ')}" if bcc.any?
headers << "Subject: #{subject}"
headers << "In-Reply-To: #{in_reply_to}" unless in_reply_to.empty?
headers << "References: #{references}" unless references.empty?
headers << "Date: #{Time.now.rfc2822}"
headers << "MIME-Version: 1.0"

if body_format == "html"
  boundary = "alt-#{SecureRandom.hex(8)}"
  plain = html_to_text_fallback.call(body)

  headers << "Content-Type: multipart/alternative; boundary=\"#{boundary}\""

  body_content = [
    "--#{boundary}",
    "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"UTF-8\"",
    "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit",
    "",
    crlf.call(plain),
    "",
    "--#{boundary}",
    "Content-Type: text/html; charset=\"UTF-8\"",
    "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit",
    "",
    crlf.call(body),
    "",
    "--#{boundary}--",
    ""
  ].join("\r\n")

  mime = (headers + ["", body_content]).join("\r\n")
else
  headers << "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"UTF-8\""
  headers << "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit"
  mime = (headers + ["", crlf.call(body)]).join("\r\n")
end

draft_request = { "message" => { "raw" => base64url_encode.call(mime) } }
draft_request["message"]["threadId"] = thread_id unless thread_id.empty?

{
  "draft_create_request" => draft_request,
  "mime_preview" => mime
}
input.name: Create email mime
